items.title: Rondje Buitenland Extra: Arsenal kraakt en verbazing over commotie Vinícius
items.summary: Kenner buitenlands voetbal Süleyman Öztürk neemt in Rondje Buitenland Extra de voetbalweek door. De titelstrijd in de Premier League is spannender dan ooit: geeft Arsenal het wéér weg? De meeste aandacht in deze speciale aflevering is echter voor al het Champions League-voetbal. Het incident met Vinícius, de prestaties van Teun Koopmeiners en Noa Lang én het succes van Bodø/Glimt komen uitgebreid ter sprake.

items.title: Prijzengeld Conference League: dit kan AZ verdienen de rest van het toernooi
items.summary: AZ is de enige nog overgebleven Nederlandse ploeg in Europa. De Alkmaarders hopen hoge ogen te gooien in de Conference League. De prijzenpot in het derde Europese toernooi ligt aanzienlijk lager dan in de Champions en Europa League. Toch strijdt AZ om de nodige miljoenen.

items.title: ‘Freak of nature’: how James Milner closed in on Premier League record
items.summary: Those who have worked with midfielder reflect on his career as he prepares to make a 654th top-flight appearance James Milner was the most dedicated and professional young player I’ve met. He also took the not inconsiderable transition from being at school to playing in the Leeds first team totally in his stride. Nothing fazed him. He was very level-headed. Continue reading...

items.title: VI Live: AZ-spelers verkennen Jerevan
items.summary: AZ vervolgt donderdagavond het Europese avontuur. De Alkmaarders staan in de tussenronde van de Conference League tegenover het Armeense FC Noah. Volgende week is de return in Alkmaar. Volg de heenwedstrijd in deze VI Live.

items.title: Lagsir (PEC) stopt op 22-jarige leeftijd, Parrott wilde bij AZ blijven
items.summary: Blijf via dit blog op de hoogte van alle transfers en transfergeruchten bij en rond clubs in de Eredivisie en de Keuken Kampioen Divisie. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in het buitenland .

items.title: Netball Super League's top three will be tough to topple, says Neville
items.summary: Sky Sports netball pundit Tracey Neville expects to see Loughborough Lightning, London Pulse and Manchester Thunder battling for honours once again in the upcoming Netball Super League season.

items.title: Champions League review: Bodø shock again, PSG escape and Mourinho’s dismal comments
items.summary: Racism allegations in Portugal overshadowed another fine result in the Arctic and the holders being pushed by their Ligue 1 rivals Nothing should divert attention away from what happened after Vinícius Júnior’s goal for Real Madrid in their 1-0 victory at Benfica on Tuesday. It would be frivolous to do so. The Brazilian scored one of the finest goals of a career marked by spectacular strikes, but this week’s Champions League action will be remembered for the regrettable flashpoint that followed. Continue reading...

items.title: Discussie over VAR onbegrepen: 'Het gaat te veel over de uitzonderingen'
items.summary: Bij Club Brugge zullen ze de VAR dankbaar zijn. Het late doelpunt tegen Atlético Madrid werd eerst door de arbitrage afgekeurd vanwege buitenspel, maar de VAR kwam op de lijn en daardoor speelden de Belgen gelijk (3-3) in de tussenronde van de Champions League. Een mooi voorbeeld van de toevoeging van de VAR, vertelt hoofdredacteur Pieter Zwart in deze video.

items.title: 'Another nonsense!' - Wolff fumes despite accepting proposed engine change
items.summary: Mercedes boss Toto Wolff says he is "philosophically" opposed to the FIA's proposal of a mid-season rule change over a contentious engine element, but says his team are willing to be "good citizens" by accepting it despite it potentially harming their performance.

items.title: Saka rejects talk of Arsenal wilting but scars of title near-misses run deep
items.summary: Late Wolves leveller means seven dropped points from winning positions in 2026 – and Manchester City are lurking It was left to Bukayo Saka to sum up the mood in Arsenal’s dressing room. “Very flat,” admitted the England forward after watching his side surrender a 2-0 lead at Wolves on Wednesday night. A couple of hours earlier, Saka’s first goal in 15 games in all competitions – his longest drought since breaking into the first team as a fresh-faced teenager in 2018 – looked to have set up an easy victory over the Premier League’s bottom side to restore Arsenal’s seven-point cushion over Manchester City. Made captain for the night by Mikel Arteta in the continued absence of Martin Ødegaard, Saka celebrated his rare headed goal by mimicking signing the lucrative five-year contract worth more than £300,000 a week that he has agreed. But his broad smile had turned to a frown by the time he faced the television cameras in the tunnel at Molineux. Continue reading...

items.title: Vinícius Jr. racism allegation prompts UEFA probe
items.summary: UEFA will investigate the racism allegations made by Vinícius Júnior in Real Madrid's game against Benfica.

items.title: Benfica: 'Smear campaign' after Viní allegations
items.summary: SL Benfica have condemned the "smear campaign" to which their player Gianluca Prestianni has been subjected to following allegations that he racially abused Real Madrid's Vinícius Júnior.

items.title: Arteta: Arsenal 'didn't show anything' vs. Wolves
items.summary: Arsenal boss Mikel Arteta said his team have only themselves to blame after surrendering a two-goal lead to draw 2-2 with rock-bottom Wolves at Molineux, adding that all criticism of his side was fair after the capitulation.

items.title: Bodo/Glimt add win over Inter to impressive run
items.summary: Champions League runner-up Inter Milan became the latest top team to suffer an upset by Bodø/Glimt in the Champions League, losing 3-1 away in the first leg of their playoff on Wednesday.

items.title: How Szoboszlai became Liverpool's key man
items.summary: Inside the Hungarian midfielder's season as the main character at Liverpool, his friendship with Mohamed Salah and why the Reds are keen to extend his contract.

items.title: Leicester appeal against six-point deduction
items.summary: Leicester City appeal against a decision to deduct them six points for breaching English Football League financial rules.

items.title: Leicester and Premier League appeal over club’s six-point deduction
items.summary: Club unhappy sanction applied in Championship Premier League seeking additional punishment Leicester City’s legal battle with the Premier League continues to rumble on as both parties formally lodged appeals related to this month’s decision by an independent commission to deduct the club six points. It is understood the league believes Leicester should be punished additionally for the late submission of their annual accounts for 2023-24, to avoid setting a precedent, and said an appeal board will “urgently” hear the case to ensure it is resolved before the end of the English Football League season in May. Continue reading...

items.title: Alexander Isak should return from injury for Liverpool in April, says Arne Slot
items.summary: £125m summer signing broke a leg in December He should rejoin team training late March or early April Arne Slot is expecting Alexander Isak to be available for a Liverpool return in April. The striker fractured his left leg and sustained an ankle injury against Tottenham in December and required surgery but is back in light training. Isak started running this week as part of his rehabilitation and if he continues on the same path, he will join team training at the end of March. “It will be around that period of time – end of March, start of April – where he is hopefully back with the group,” the head coach said. Continue reading...

items.title: Potential game-changer? Ferrari's 'clever' upside-down rear wing spotted in testing
items.summary: Technical analyst Sam Collins examines Ferrari's innovative upside-down rear wing during the second day of the final test in Bahrain.

items.title: Champions League talking points: PSG's comeback win, Bodø/Glimt surprise Inter, more
items.summary: We had a very entertaining week of first leg knockout playoff matches in UEFA Champions League! ESPN FC's writers pick out their highs and lows of the tournament to this point.
